description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant, painted in a bright red color, commonly found in urban environments for emergency water access. The hydrant features a domed top with a central operating nut, flanked by two large side outlets and a smaller front outlet, all capped with hexagonal covers. The base is wide and ribbed for stability, and the body is vertically ribbed for structural strength. The outlets are designed for hose attachment, and the central nut is used to operate the internal valve. There are no visible chains or manufacturer markings on the exterior. The hydrant's robust construction suggests it is made primarily of cast iron or ductile iron, designed to withstand high pressure and outdoor conditions.
description: A bright red, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top, three capped outlets, and a ribbed base and body. It is robustly built for emergency water access in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed top, central operating nut, and all three capped outlets, with the front outlet most prominent.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the right-side outlet and the central nut, with the cylindrical body and ribbed base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, with both side outlets visible but the front outlet and nut less prominent.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, showing the left-side outlet and the central nut, similar to view 1 but mirrored.
